description_long: This is a cylindrical fire hydrant with a vertically ribbed body and a domed top, both showing significant weathering and rust. The top dome is painted white, with visible rust streaks and a central bronze-colored operating nut. The body is predominantly dark, almost black, with patches of white paint and rust, indicating long-term outdoor exposure. There are two large hose connection outlets on the sides and one smaller outlet facing forward, each capped with a metal cover. The hydrant's surface is rough and matte, with visible corrosion and chipped paint, giving it a rugged, utilitarian appearance. No chains or manufacturer markings are visible, and the hydrant appears to be made primarily of cast iron.
description: A weathered, dark fire hydrant with a white domed top, rusted metal caps, and a rough, ribbed cylindrical body. The hydrant shows signs of age and outdoor exposure.
description_view_0: Front view showing the white domed top, central operating nut, and a smaller hose outlet protruding from the left side.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the larger hose outlet and cap, with the domed top and rust streaks clearly visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the largest hose outlet and cap, with the ribbed body and weathered paint most prominent.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing another large hose outlet and cap, with the domed top and rust details visible.
